items.title: The Milwaukee Bucks have agreed to sign 2025 second-round pick Kam Jones on a two-way NBA deal, sources tell ESPN. Jones was traded from Indiana to Chicago on draft night before being released, and now joins the Bucks.
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt: The Milwaukee Bucks have agreed to sign 2025 second-round pick Kam Jones on a two-way NBA deal, sources tell ESPN. Jones was traded from Indiana to Chicago on draft night before being released, and now joins the Bucks.

items.title: The Milwaukee Bucks and Kam Jones have agreed to a two-way deal, league sources told HoopsHype. Jones was the 38th pick of the 2025 NBA Draft. Agent Kevin Bradbury of LIFT Sports Management worked with the Bucks to land Jones in Milwaukee.
